question_text: A wind turbine receives 500,000 J of wind energy per second. Due to mechanical friction and electrical losses, only 40% of this becomes electrical energy. A transmission system then loses 15% of the electrical energy as heat in the cables. How much electrical energy finally reaches the consumer?
explanation: First, calculate electrical energy produced: 40% of 500,000 = 200,000 J. Then subtract transmission losses: 15% of 200,000 = 30,000 J lost, so 200,000 - 30,000 = 170,000 J reaches the consumer. This multi-step problem shows how energy losses accumulate in real systems.
